title: Stock Market Today: Dow Futures Tumble, S&P 500, Nasdaq Gains As Investors Await FOMC Decision— Amkor, La-Z-Boy In Focus (UPDATED)
author: Rishabh Mishra
description: U.S. stock futures showed mixed performance on Wednesday with the Dow Jones declining while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 advanced. Investors awaited the FOMC interest rate decision expected at 2:00 p.m. ET, with markets pricing a 99.6% likelihood of unchanged rates. Notable movers included La-Z-Boy surging 19.62% on strong earnings and a $300 million buyback, Amkor jumping 5.49% after signing a 10-year deal with TSMC, and SpaceX rising 3.22% following its Cursor AI acquisition.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Surged 19.62% after reporting better-than-expected Q4 results and announcing a $300 million share buyback program, indicating strong financial performance and shareholder returns
Jumped 5.49% following a 10-year strategic deal with TSMC to expand advanced semiconductor packaging in Arizona, signaling strong business growth and long-term partnership
Rose 3.22% ahead of its fourth trading day following the acquisition of Cursor AI, demonstrating investor confidence in the AI expansion strategy
Fell 3.45% in premarket after announcing a proposed public offering, which typically dilutes existing shareholder value
Dropped 8.09% despite announcing debt restructuring agreements, suggesting market skepticism about the company's financial health despite management's positive framing
Up 2.11% after announcing a global partnership with Uber and Wayve for Level 4 driverless robotaxis development, modest gain reflects cautious market sentiment on autonomous vehicle prospects
Declined 0.14% despite announcing the robotaxi partnership, indicating the market has already priced in expectations or has limited enthusiasm for this development

title: Why Is Amkor Technology Stock Soaring Wednesday?
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Amkor Technology stock surged nearly 6% in premarket trading following a 10-year partnership announcement with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. to expand advanced semiconductor packaging and testing capacity in Arizona. The collaboration aims to strengthen the U.S. semiconductor supply chain for AI and high-performance computing chips. Amkor's stock has gained 325.94% over the past year and trades near its 52-week high, though analysts maintain a consensus Hold rating with an average price target of $69.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock surged 5.68% on partnership announcement with TSMC; strong technical momentum with 325.94% year-over-year gain; trading above all major moving averages; positive MACD indicators; however, elevated valuation (49.7x earnings) and extended chart suggest pullback risk

Announced strategic 10-year partnership to expand U.S. semiconductor capacity; collaboration strengthens domestic supply chain and supports company's Arizona facility development; demonstrates commitment to advanced packaging and testing capabilities

title: Nasdaq 100 Hits Record Above 30,000 On Micron's 18% Rally: Stock Market Today
author: Piero Cingari
description: The Nasdaq 100 crossed 30,000 during intraday trading as semiconductor stocks surged, led by Micron Technology's 18% gain following a UBS analyst price target hike to $1,625. The rally was driven by increased AI spending expectations from hyperscalers, with chip stocks dominating market leadership. The S&P 500 rose 0.6%, while the Russell 2000 outperformed with a 1.7% gain. AutoZone plunged 10.1% after missing revenue estimates despite beating EPS.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock surged 18% on UBS analyst price target hike to $1,625 (from $535), driven by strong AI memory demand expectations. Market cap exceeded $1 trillion for the first time, with stock up 200% year-to-date.
Jumped 12.3% tracking the broader semiconductor rally and benefiting from announced cash dividend, with no company-specific negative catalyst.
Rose 9.1% on sympathy buying with the semiconductor sector rotation, driven by positive AI infrastructure spending sentiment.
Climbed 8.7% lifted by the chip sector rotation and increased AI-related spending expectations.
Added 8.4% as part of the broader semiconductor and tech sector rally driven by AI infrastructure demand.
Gained 6.8% as cheaper jet fuel and Iran deal narrative boosted airline stocks, lifting the broader airline sector.
Plunged 10.1% despite beating EPS estimates, due to revenue miss ($4.84B vs $4.88B estimate) and soft domestic same-store sales of 4.1% against elevated expectations.
Dropped 6% as investors trimmed positions ahead of May 28 earnings announcement.

title: The Real SpaceX Play: 5 Chip Stocks Powering the IPO Before It Launches
author: Bridget Bennett
description: As SpaceX prepares for a $2 trillion IPO, the semiconductor companies powering its technology are already publicly traded. The article highlights five chip stocks benefiting from SpaceX's infrastructure needs and the broader shift toward agentic AI, which is increasing CPU demand alongside GPU demand. Key beneficiaries include TSMC (manufacturing backbone), Intel (CPU demand surge), AMD (CPU and GPU exposure), NVIDIA (consolidating before next growth phase), and Amkor Technology (advanced packaging services).

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Described as the manufacturing backbone of the AI chip ecosystem with dominant position in advanced semiconductor fabrication. Wins regardless of which company designs chips, with strengthening moat as chip complexity increases.

Benefits from structural shift in CPU-to-GPU demand ratio (compressing from 8-to-1 to 4-to-1) driven by agentic AI workloads. Stock up over 100% in past month with underlying thesis described as structural rather than speculative.
Positioned to benefit from both CPU demand surge and GPU exposure. Part of infrastructure stack for agentic AI tools being explored by 90% of Fortune 500 companies.

Currently consolidating after GPU narrative plateau, but positioned for new demand cycle from agentic AI wave. Described as having unmatched GPU pricing power and market position with significant upside potential.

Semiconductor packaging company benefiting from shift to chiplet designs requiring sophisticated assembly. Increasing value-add and supply chain leverage. Stock up 65% before recent pullback, with consolidation viewed as normal technical reset.

title: Amkor Stock Climbs On Strong Q1 Earnings: Details
author: Erica Kollmann
description: Amkor Technology (NASDAQ: AMKR) reported strong Q1 2026 results, beating analyst expectations with EPS of 33 cents versus 22 cents consensus and revenue of $1.69 billion versus $1.65 billion estimate. The company also provided optimistic Q2 guidance of 42-52 cents EPS and $1.75-1.85 billion revenue, both above analyst expectations. Stock rose 0.94% to $76.30 in extended trading.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company beat both EPS and revenue estimates in Q1, demonstrated record quarterly revenue with year-over-year growth, and provided forward guidance significantly above analyst expectations for Q2, indicating strong operational execution and market demand.
